findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of the PDF page 1 is not present in the HTML rendering on page 2.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the Great Seal of North Carolina centered at the top of the page. The HTML page 2 rendering does not display this seal.

findings.description: Page 2 of the HTML has a large blank space occupying most of the page before the DEQ logo and header information appear, resulting in poor layout fidelity.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 content is compact and well-structured. HTML page 2 shows extensive empty whitespace before the DEQ branding and the re-rendering of the agenda content.

findings.description: The HTML rendering duplicates the entire agenda content. Page 1 of the HTML shows the agenda, and then pages 2-3 repeat it again in a different format, creating redundant content.
findings.evidence: PDF has one copy of the agenda on page 1. HTML pages 2-3 repeat the same agenda content that already appeared on HTML page 1, leading to duplicated information.

findings.description: The state seal image present in the PDF header on page 1 is missing from the HTML rendering of the same header on page 2.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 top header has the NC state seal. HTML page 2 header area shows the JOSH STEIN, D. REID WILSON text but has a smaller/different version of the seal or it's absent.

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> elements and uses h2 for 'WebEx Meeting Information' which is reasonable, but 'NC DEQ Secretaries' Science Advisory Board Meeting' is rendered as h3, which creates an inconsistent hierarchy. More importantly, there are multiple <main> elements nested inside each other, which is invalid HTML structure.
findings.evidence: The outer <body> contains a <main>, which itself contains two more <main> elements for page 1 and page 2 content.

findings.description: The page footer content (DEQ address and phone number) appears twice in the HTML, once for each page. This mirrors the PDF where it appears on both pages, so it is a faithful reproduction but could be considered structural duplication in a single-document HTML output.
findings.evidence: Footer block with '217 West Jones Street | 1601 Mail Service Center | Raleigh, North Carolina 27699-1601' and '919-707-8700' appears twice.
